name: KGS Hauler IV
description: Although the Hauler, Hauler II, and Hauler III are more popular, the Hauler IV is still used by many, for its low price, durable hull, and excellent cargo space. It is, however, very slow.

name: KGS Hauler V
description: The Hauler V has even more cargo space than the Hauler IV, but it is even slower. It is, however, very durable.

name: KGS Hauler VI
description: The Hauler VI has an incredible amount of cargo space, but it is equally slow. It is only used because it is cheaper than two Hauler IIIs.

name: KGS Hauler VII
description: The Hauler VII is the largest freighter built by Southbound Shipyards. It lives up to its title with over 900 tons of cargo space, while being cheaper than multiple smaller freighters.

name: KGS Maker
description: A Rainmaker without its missile launchers, the Maker is a sturdy but sluggish interceptor. Because of its decreased firepower, the Maker is available to regular civilians for purchase.

name: KGS Dipper
description: The Dipper is a light warship and freighter cobbled together from two Clipper hulls. Seven gun ports and a spacious cargo hold make it a desirable ship among merchants and pirates in the South.

name: KGS Mistmaker
description: The Mistmaker is lighter version of the Rainmaker missile boat, built for civilian use. It comes equipped with four missile pods and enough ammunition to destroy even ships many times its size, and is also slightly faster than the Rainmaker.

name: KGS Nightingale
description: The Nightingale is a modified Raven with 24 gun ports. Such a huge modification resulted in lowered shield efficiency and hull structural integrity, along with higher drag and worse heat dissipation.

name: KGS Sparrowhawk
description: Tarazed's Sparrowhawk-class light warship was made by mashing together their interceptors, the Hawk and the Sparrow. The result is a powerful ship, with six guns and decent mobility. It has become very popular as an affordable escort for captains fending off pirates in the South.

name: KGS BIG PEW PEW
description: The Lionheart Raven is their most popular warship, an agile and elegant vessel that can nevertheless deal out and absorb considerable damage. Ravens are a favorite ship for bounty hunters, because they are more than a match for pirate interceptors and can be used by a skilled pilot to take down much larger ships as well.

name: KGS Eyas
description: A shortened Kingfisher, the Eyas-class Destroyer is the fastest medium warship in the Southern Rim, although it is somewhat undergunned.

name: KGS Eyrie
description: The Lionheart Eyrie is a light carrier derived from the Aerie. The Aerie's four internal fighter bays have been rearranged into six drone bays, which are commonly found carrying Lionheart's Stiletto drones.

name: KGS Stormmaker
description: The Stormmaker is a heavy missile platform derived from the Rainmaker missile boat. It comes equipped with twelve missile launchers and enough ammunition to destroy ships many times its size. However, it is still slow, and its backup non-missile armament is still very weak.

name: KGS Kingfisher
description: A Falcon without its side pods, the Kingfisher-class Battlecruiser is faster and even sleeker, at the cost of weapon space and hardpoints. It has been adapted to use strike tactics, flying fast at the target and unloading a flurry of plasma fire and Javelins before retreating for another dive.

name: KGS Kookaburra
description: Tarazed Shipyards created this monstrosity as a competitor to the Syndicate Protector. A Kingfisher has been stretched, and 10 extra turrets have been added. What it lacks in affordability it makes up for in mobility - upgraded engines allow it to go alarmingly fast for a 12 turret ship.

name: KGS Fallcoon
description: The Tarazed Fallcoon was designed as a competitor to Southbound Shipyards' Dreadnought. Its twin Falcon hulls boast huge engine bays and a staggering array of Plasma Cannons. Although its design was completed too late for it to take part in the civil war, it is still a terrifying warship, able to go head-to-head with a Cruiser.

name: KGS Raindrop
description: The Raindrop is a light missile drone built in the Republic Navy Yard, equipped with two missile launchers. Because of its extremely frail hull and lack of shielding, Raindrops serve best as long-range attack drones, and once their missile supply is spent, they are useless in combat.

name: KGS Stiletto
description: The Stiletto is a drone developed by Lionheart, derived from the Dagger fighter. As with almost all Lionheart ships, its hull is very light. A potent shield matrix along with long range missile pods give it above average survivability for a drone. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

name: KGS Yestrel
description: Two years after the design of the Kestrel, Tarazed's chief ship designer has finally completed the design of a ship even more powerful, the Yestrel.

name: KGS Biolofez
description: The Biolofez was made by integrating the hulls of two salvaged 'olofez. The result is a quite capable interceptor. Due to its low fuel capacity and lack of shield generation, the Remnant use it only for patrol and point defense.

name: KGS Triolofez
description: A Biolofez with another 'olofez tacked on, the Triolofez is a very powerful interceptor. However, its low fuel capacity combined with the fact that the Remnant do not make dedicated shield generation or hull repair have relegated it to in-system patrol and point defense.

name: KGS Yolofez
description: The Yolofez is two 'olofez hulls put together, but with the cockpit of the upper 'olofez modified into a turret mount. The result is a good interceptor. However, due to its low fuel capacity and lack of recovery systems, the Remnant use it only for patrol and point defense.

name: KGS Silver Penguin
description: Inspired by close contact with void sprites and the reinvigoration of xenobiology, the Penguin is a prototype designed to serve the dual roles of testing new starship technology while exploring hostile planets throughout the Ember Waste. One of this ship's distinguishing features is that it is truly alive and has a mind that approaches self-awareness. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Penguin's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: KGS Silver Puffin
description: Centuries ago, the Remnant built these ships in order to explore the upper atmosphere of nearby gas giants. The Archons took issue with the Remnant's research methods, and the studies were unable to be completed. The remaining Puffins have been gathering dust in storage for centuries while the Remnant wait to see if the anger of the Archons will subside.

name: KGS Silver Gull
description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: KGS Silver Pelican
description: The Pelican is closely based on the original semi-organic materials the Remnant found in the Ember Waste. While they are not technically alive, their internal mechanisms and method of construction bear such a close resemblance to living organisms that they are often considered as such. One key feature of this living resemblance is the innate sensory capabilities they possess, akin to sensor suites less advanced ships require for detailed scanning. These ships serve as the backbone of Remnant bulk salvage efforts, as well as their fast-logistics distribution network.

name: KGS Silver Merganser
description: The Merganser is the first production ship the Remnant deem to be truly alive, and are considered to be equivalent to dolphins in reasoning tests. These interceptors, deemed for short range operations by Remnant standards, are designed to patrol the upper atmosphere of gas giants and use their decisive speed advantage to conduct hit-and-run attacks on threats in nearby systems. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Merganser's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: KGS Silver Starling
description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: KGS Silver Ibis
description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

name: KGS Silver Peregrine
description: The Peregrine is the first capital warship designed by Taely using fresh insights garnered from recent experimentation as well as renewed studies of the void sprites. Encompassing over a century of development since the last generation of warships was built, it is the forerunner of a new breed of Remnant ships. 	The Peregrine was designed along the lines of old-earth's fast-attack cruisers. Drawing on inspiration from its namesake, famed for being the fastest self-propelled lifeform on Earth, it is designed to strike hard and fast. It closes quickly with enemies to unleash devastating barrages in the opening seconds of combat. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Peregrine's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: KGS Silver Albatross
description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

name: KGS Silver Petrel
description: The Petrel is a multi-role mining/wing leader fightercraft designed to provide support for flights of lighter Terns in high-risk environments. The ship's design originated as an urban mobile gun platform that flitted around structures and natural terrain, but proved easy to adapt into a spacecraft. 	The Remnant are loath to sacrifice pilots and crew from the comparatively small pool they are drawn from. As such, their fighters are equipped with medium-range weaponry designed to strike enemies from afar. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: KGS Silver Smew
description: The Remnant have long favored turns of action that avoid putting crew directly at risk; though their Terns are a rather effective result of this ideology, the Remnant still had to face the fact that, eventually, one of their manned ships was going to end up too crippled to escape to safety. To that end, the Smew was developed; though quite lightly armed, Smews are capable of carrying a considerable quantity of cargo and passengers for a ship of their size, and are durable enough to absorb some fire from enemies while doing so. 	Though originally designed as lifeboats, the surprisingly mobile freighter-fighter hybrid has seen a remarkable amount of usage in mining fleets, as a way to supplement the otherwise poor cargo capacity of the Ibis. Because their purpose can vary from fleet to fleet, Smews are traditionally sold with a fair amount of free space: enough for a small weapon, upgraded engines, or additional support systems. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: KGS Silver Tern
description: The Tern is a small, hardy drone developed to plunge into the atmosphere of nearby gas giants for collecting valuable resources. These thick-shelled craft are also surprisingly effective when pressed into service as screening ships when enemies intrude into Remnant space. 	Unlike most spacefaring nations, the Remnant do not have enough people to pilot fighter wings in waves likely to sustain significant casualties. As such, they frequently use these hardy drones to fulfill similar screening roles in their home systems, allowing manned craft to focus on more specialized tasks. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

name: KGS Rapid Stream
description: Developed after the Unfettered invasion, the Rapid Stream is a cut down Deep River, equipped with three turrets for extra defense from Unfettered Trackers.
